channel.title: Tech Insights
channel.description: Daily curated news on AI, developer tools, and the tech industry

item.title: AI Didn’t Make Me a Worse Developer. It Made Me Realize I Was Already One.
item.description: Few months ago, AI felt like a superpower. I was shipping features faster than ever, smashing bugs in minutes and building side projects on a coffee break. Then I noticed something strange: I was...

item.title: Claude Fable 5 Is Two Models Wearing One Name
item.description: On June 9, 2026, Anthropic shipped the most capable model it has ever released to the public. The most interesting thing about it is the part that sometimes refuses to talk to you. Claude Fable 5...

item.title: Flutter Midsommer Madnesss
item.description: This is a submission for the June Solstice Game Jam When you have a hammer- everything is a nail! This installment brings a Flutter build to Midsommer Madness via Antigravity. A complete Android...

item.title: Vortex 3.0 RISC-V GPGPU, Pragtical SDL GPU Backend, NVIDIA RTX Spark Launch
item.description: Vortex 3.0 RISC-V GPGPU, Pragtical SDL GPU Backend, NVIDIA RTX Spark Launch Today's Highlights Today's top stories highlight significant advancements in open-source GPU hardware with Vortex 3.0...

item.title: Congress just gave DHS another $70 billion
item.description: Congress narrowly voted to fund President Donald Trump's mass deportation agenda, giving the Department of Homeland Security $70 billion over the next three years. The house voted 214 to 212 in...

item.title: The AI-generated C# that passes review and breaks in production
item.description: TL;DR — AI assistants are producing C# that looks correct and passes review, but reintroduces production regressions we spent years training out of teams. I'm trying to find out whether other .NET...

item.title: The App Store is going to add subscription bundles soon
item.description: Later this year, you will be able to get bundled subscriptions for iPhone apps, as Apple announced it's expanding App Store bundles so they can include offers from different companies. It's...

item.title: Local Time, UTC, Offset και Epoch: Ο απόλυτος οδηγός για developers
item.description: Το πρόβλημα της ώρας Η ώρα είναι από τα πιο ύπουλα προβλήματα στην ανάπτυξη λογισμικού. Αν ένας χρήστης στην Αθήνα δημιουργήσει μια παραγγελία στις 20:00 και ένας άλλος στη Νέα Υόρκη τη δει στις...

item.title: How to Compare Testing Tools Without Getting Fooled by Feature Checklists
item.description: The biggest mistake teams make when comparing testing tools is treating the feature list like the decision. A tool can support API tests, visual checks, CI, reporting, and integrations, and still...

item.title: A practical playbook for choosing browser automation and cross-browser testing tools
item.description: If your goal is faster releases with fewer flaky failures, the tool choice matters less than the testing strategy behind it. Teams usually start by asking, “Should we use Playwright, Selenium,...

item.title: I Added x402 Payments to Base's Agent Skills — Here's How
item.description: If you build agents on Base, two things landed recently that are worth connecting. First: Base shipped its own Agent Skills. There's now a base/skills repo with consolidated skills that teach an...

item.title: Deploying a Dockerized Node.js Application on Kubernetes 🚀
item.description: After containerizing an application with Docker, the next logical step is deploying it on Kubernetes. Kubernetes helps automate application deployment, scaling, networking, and management of...

item.title: GM thinks EVs can help offset AI’s energy suck with vehicle-to-grid tech
item.description: At an event in San Francisco today, General Motors made a series of announcements around EV batteries, energy storage, and grid resiliency in the face of growing electricity demand from AI data...

item.title: Hey Siri, here’s what I actually want from AI
item.description: I'm desperate for a personal AI assistant, but do I really want to become the kind of person who can't function without the friendly robot voice in my phone?

item.title: Anthropic’s Fable 5 can make weirdly fun video games with the click of a button
item.description: Anthropic's Claude Fable 5 is going to be a big hit with the web's vibe coders.

item.title: Microsoft AI head calls out Anthropic for acting like Claude is conscious
item.description: Microsoft AI CEO Mustafa Suleyman says it's "really, really dangerous" for Anthropic to speculate about Claude's consciousness inside its "constitution," or the instructions that tell the model...

item.title: Can Voice Agents Handle Bilingual Customers? Benchmarking Frontier ASR on Code-Switched Speech
Nintendo is playing things too safe
item.description: Tuesday's Nintendo Direct showcase felt like an important moment for the company. With the Switch 2 heading into its second holiday season, one in which the hardware will be even more expensive...

item.title: Can tech companies learn to love cheaper AI models?
item.description: If those same AI workloads can be handled by cheaper models without affecting quality, it would mean a massive shift in the economics of AI.

item.title: WWDC 2026: Everything announced on Siri AI, iOS 27, Apple Intelligence, and more
item.description: Apple primarily made the case for an improved experience with its long-standing Siri assistant, which like most other announcements had a hefty helping of AI.

item.title: Fitbit’s Charge 6 and Ace LTE are now as cheap as the new $100 Air
item.description: Whether you’re shopping for Father’s Day or trying to keep your kids entertained over summer break, you don’t need to spend a fortune to get a great Fitbit right now. You can currently pick up the...

item.title: Apple wants Europe to blink
item.description: It took a few years, but Apple finally made its AI look useful. Now millions of iPhone users in Europe are being told they won't be getting Siri AI anytime soon, if ever - and Apple wants them to...

item.title: Meta to Use Off-Site Business Data for Feed and AI Personalization
item.description: Meta on Tuesday announced that it will use information shared by other businesses to personalize users' feed and responses from its artificial intelligence (AI) chatbot, expanding its scope beyond...

item.title: Anthropic Offers Mythos Upgrade for Cyber Partners and a ‘Safe’ Version for the Rest of You
item.description: Anthropic is releasing Claude Mythos 5 to trusted organizations and Claude Fable 5 to the public, a version it says can’t be used for cyberattacks.

item.title: Anthropic’s Claude Fable 5 is a version of Mythos the public can access today
item.description: Anthropic is releasing Claude Fable 5, its first Mythos-class model available to the public. The model comes with guardrails that block responses in high-risk areas like cybersecurity and biology.

item.title: Anthropic releases its first Mythos-class model Claude Fable
item.description: Anthropic just announced Claude Fable 5, a new AI model it said is the most powerful model it has ever made widely available. According to the company, Fable 5 "shows exceptional performance in...

item.title: SwitchBot’s E Ink Weather Station is already 20 percent off
item.description: We’re only a few days since the SwitchBot Smart E Ink Weather Station launched, and you can already save $25 on the smart home gadget. Visit Amazon and clip the on-page coupon to bring the price...

item.title: Veeam Backup & Replication RCE Flaw Lets Domain Users Run Remote Code
item.description: Veeam has released security patches to address a critical flaw in its Backup & Replication software that could result in remote code execution. Tracked as CVE-2026-44963, the vulnerability carries...

item.title: Microsoft Restores Some GitHub Repos, Keeps Others Offline as Miasma Probe Continues
item.description: Microsoft on Monday confirmed that it temporarily removed some GitHub repositories in response to a recent security incident that led to 73 of its open-source projects being compromised to inject...

item.title: Apple is embracing the fantasy of AI photo editing
item.description: Apple used to question whether generative AI-powered editing features were worth the risk of distorting our perceptions of the world. Now it seems Apple no longer believes that photos should...
